english: tame female yak; rgod-°bri (rgod 'bri) Pth., or °broṅ-°bri ('brong 'bri) Cs., wild female yak; °bri-zal ('bri zal) young female yak Ld.-Glr., °bri-o ('bri 'o) yak-milk; °bri-mar ('bri mar) yak-butter; °bri-mdzo ('bri mdzo) (W. *brim-dzo* (*brim dzo*)) bastard of bull and yak.

middle, midst, mean, middling, moderate, °briṅ źig ('bring zhig) something moderate, of middling quality, = tsád-ma (tshad ma) or tig-tsád (tig tshad) W.; briṅ-po ('bring po) the middle one, of three sons Dzl. and elsewh.; between stobs-c'é (stobs che) and c'uṅ-ṅu (chung ngu) Lt.; bzaṅ ṅan °briṅ γsum (bzang ngan 'bring gsum); rnal-°byór °briṅ-po (rnal 'byor 'bring po) one that is moderately advanced in contemplation Thgr.; zlá-ba °briṅ-po (zla ba 'bring po) v. zla-ba (zla ba); °briṅ-gis ('bring gis) middling, moderately, adv.
in źabs-°briṅ byéd-pa (zhabs 'bring byed pa) for bráṅ-ba (brang ba) Mil.

1. also °drid-pa ('drid pa), pf. brid (brid), to deceive, cheat, impose upon, blo °brid-pa (blo 'brid pa) id. Glr.; °brid-de rṅód-pa-las ('brid de rngod pa las) Tar., as she wanted to seduce him deceitfully; ka-mṅar-brid (kha mngar brid) deceitfully, insidiously sweet, being followed by a nauseous, acrid or burning taste Med. — 2. Cs. = °p'ri-ba ('phri ba).

I. vb., pf. brim(s) (brim(s)) 1. to distribute, deal out, hand round, sweet-meats, flowers, poems Dzl., Tar.; ... la, to ... — 2. Ld. to throw away, what is worthless, = *p'áṅ-c'es* (*phang ches*). — II. sbst. distributer, dispenser, waiter at table Dzl.; °brim(-pa)-po ('brim (pa) po), id. Cs.

grain, corn, seed, frq.; grain of sand, byé-ma °bru reí stéṅ-na (bye ma 'bru re'i steng na) on every grain of sand Glr.; °bru °t'ag-pa ('bru 'thag pa) to pound grains Lex. — 2. a single grain, piece, letter, yi-ge °bru γcig (yi ge 'bru gcig) a single letter; also without yi-ge (yi ge): °bru drúg ('bru drug) the six letters = yi-ge-drúg-pa (yi ge drug pa), v. drug (drug). — 3. collectively, grain, corn, in gen. °bruí k'al ('bru'i khal) a load of grain Dzl.; °brú-sna mi °k'ruṅs ('bru sna mi 'khrungs) no kind of grain is growing Glr.; °bru gaṅ-bu-c'an ('bru gang bu can) pulse, legume S.g.; nor daṅ °bru-rnams °p'el (nor dang 'bru rnams 'phel) money and corn multiply. — °bru-rdóg ('bru rdog) grain of seed. — °brú-sna ('bru sna) v. above. — °bru-báṅ ('bru bang) granary. — °bru-°bú ('bru 'bu) corn-worm, weevil Cs. bru-már (bru mar) oil extracted from seeds; lamp-oil Dzl. — °brú-ts'oṅ-pa ('bru tshong pa) oil-merchant.
n. of a superior sort of tea.

pf. and imp. brus (brus), drú-ba (dru ba), drus (drus) 1. to dig, k'uṅ-bu (khung bu), dur (dur), doṅ (dong) (cf. rkó-ba (rko ba)). — 2. to chisel, carve, cut. — 3. Sch. to look through, yig (yig) a writing; to examine, °bru ('bru) grain; hence mtsaṅ °bru-ba (mtshang 'bru ba) to spy out, smell out, faults, stirring up brawls and quarrels by it, Stg. to irritate, vex, provoke, mtsaṅ °brú-baí tsig (mtshang 'bru ba'i tshig) provoking words Lex.; snyad (snyad), snyon °brú-ba (snyon 'bru ba) to accuse W.
an angular kind of Tibetan current handwriting, v. Csoma Gram.

(Bal. *blug* (*blug*)) 1. thunder, °brug-skád ('brug skad), °brug-sgrá ('brug sgra) id.; skad-c'én °brug (skad chen 'brug) loud thunder; °brug °bód-pa ('brug 'bod pa) Cs., grág-pa (grag pa) Dzl., ldir-ba (ldir ba) Lex. and elsewh., thundering. — 2. dragon (to which thunder is ascribed Sch.); γyu-°brúg sṅón-po (g.yu 'brug sngon po) blue dragon Glr.

I. sbst. 1. sect of Lamas, clothed in red, Schl. 73., established in the province of Bhotan, acc. to Sch. = źa-dmár (zha dmar), = sá-skya (sa skya). — 2. Bhotan. — II. vb. for °brúb-pa ('brub pa) Mil. frq.

1. gen. with c'u (chu), to cause to overflow, to gush, to spout forth to flow over, Mil., Tar. and elsewh.; c'u-°brub (chu 'brub) Lex., °brubs ('brubs) Sch. water that has flown over (?). °brub-po ('brub po) fluid, liquid; fluidity, a fluid, Cs. (?). — 2. Cs. to deal out. — 3. Sch. to shut up, wrap up.

1. Cs. grain, minute particle, brum-rdog (brum rdog), °bru-rdóg ('bru rdog) a single grain, = °bru ('bru); fruit, rgun-°brúm (rgun 'brum) grape; se-°brúm (se 'brum) hip (fruit of wild brier) Sik. — 2. pustule, pock, gen. °brúm-bu ('brum bu); °brum-nad ('brum nad) small-pox; °brum-nág ('brum nag) black or deadly small-pox; °brum-dkár ('brum dkar) white small-pox; °brum-k'rá ('brum khra) coloured small-pox Med., °brum- <
